name: Kerchunk (Cloud-Optimized Virtual Datasets)

auth.notes: No auth for local; cloud storage auth via fsspec (s3fs, gcsfs)

performance.notes: Chunk-indexed access; eliminates need to download full files; network-bound

agent_readiness.notes: Create virtual Zarr views over NetCDF4/HDF5/GRIB2 files for cloud-efficient access. from kerchunk.hdf import SingleHdf5ToZarr. out = SingleHdf5ToZarr('file.nc').translate(). import ujson; with open('ref.json', 'w') as f: ujson.dump(out, f). Open with xarray: mapper = fsspec.get_mapper('reference://', fo='ref.json'). ds = xr.open_zarr(mapper). Combine: MultiZarrToZarr for many files. GRIB2: kerchunk.grib2. Zarr v3 reference filesystem. Enables Pangeo-style cloud data access.

gotchas: Complex setup — requires understanding of fsspec, Zarr, and reference filesystems
Only works well for chunked NetCDF4/HDF5 files — unchunked files get no benefit
Still evolving — API changes between versions
Generating reference files for large datasets can be slow
best_when: Cloud-optimized access to NetCDF4/HDF5 archives without conversion
Pangeo / climate science workflows reading many NetCDF files from S3
Creating virtual Zarr stores over existing HDF5 datasets
When converting to Zarr is too expensive but cloud access is needed
avoid_when: Local-only workflows where direct xarray/NetCDF4 access works
Small datasets where overhead isn't justified
When native Zarr format is available (skip kerchunk)
summary: Kerchunk creates virtual Zarr views over NetCDF4/HDF5/GRIB2 files for cloud-efficient access. SingleHdf5ToZarr('file.nc').translate() → reference JSON. Open with xr.open_zarr(fsspec.get_mapper('reference://', fo='ref.json')). MultiZarrToZarr for multi-file aggregation. MIT by fsspec/Martin Durant. Enables Pangeo cloud workflows without converting legacy NetCDF archives to Zarr. Complex setup; files must be chunked for benefit.
